paper_title: Analysis of Transgene Integration in P. tricornutum Using Oxford Nanopore Sequencing

methods.method_name: Oxford Nanopore Sequencing for Transgene Integration Analysis
methods.description: This method uses Oxford Nanopore sequencing to analyze transgene integration in P. tricornutum cell lines. It aims to explore integrated transgene arrangements, integration locations, and associated genetic architecture.

steps.description: Apply Oxford Nanopore sequencing to interrogate bacteria-conjugated and biolistic-bombarded transgenic P. tricornutum cell lines.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Oxford Nanopore sequencing on transgenic P. tricornutum cell lines

steps.description: Assess all reads for alignment to the pPtPBR11_AP1p_CrGES-mVenus episome DNA for EE lines and to the RICE plasmid pUC19_AP1p_CrGES-mVenus for RICE lines.
steps.searchable_summary: align sequencing reads to episome and plasmid DNA

steps.description: Align reads with hits to exogenous DNA constructs to the wild type P. tricornutum genome to identify genomic integration events.
steps.searchable_summary: align reads to wild type genome to find integration events

steps.description: Summarize the results of the Oxford Nanopore sequencing analysis, including genome coverage and integration event identification.
